body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: storage layer formalization
body.intention: I intend that storage split consequences are observed and silent regressions caught before they compound.
body.description:
**What changed:** Storage formalized into clan (bootstrap only), devlab (operational), library (curated), instance.igor, employer.akien; cc_queue.py writes to devlab instead of clan.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- context-load reads clan.memories for session/decision context — if records moved to devlab, briefing goes silent
- day-close-audit schema check hardcodes clan table names — will fail if expected tables move
- Any tool querying clan.memories by kind=ticket will miss new tickets written to devlab
- Granny dispatch reads ticket queue — must still see all records after writer switch
- palace.days/sessions in adc.palace may reference clan record IDs that no longer resolve

**Signals to watch:** context-load briefing completeness (does it surface recent tickets after T-ccqueue-devlab-writer ships?); Granny dispatch succeeding; day-close-audit schema check exit code 0; cc_queue.py list showing correct total count.

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-28 or when T-ccqueue-devlab-writer closes, whichever comes first.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** no tests — observation ticket
